item.title: Lufthansa’s 100th Birthday Bash Leaves Little Cause for Cheers
item.description: Deutsche Lufthansa AG’s 100th anniversary bash this week had all the elements of a picture-perfect party. The sun was out, flight attendants in polished vintage uniforms greeted guests, and Chief Executive Officer Carsten Spohr beamed on stage with German Chancellor Friedrich Merz.

item.title: Bangladesh Continues Talks with IMF on Key Reforms Tied to Loan
item.description: Bangladesh said it will continue negotiating with the International Monetary Fund on key reforms the IMF has set as conditions for the balance of a $5.5 billion loan, with Finance Minister Amir Khosru Mahmud Chowdhury holding meetings in Washington with the global lender’s officials.

item.title: Greek, Indian Tankers U-Turn Before Hormuz Amid Reopening Doubt
item.description: Several oil tankers have u-turned in the Persian Gulf after appearing to try to transit the Strait of Hormuz, as shipowners and oil traders remain in a state of disarray as they try to figure out whether Iran would stick to its promise to keep the chokepoint open for all.

item.title: Blaze Erupts at Russia’s Baltic Port After Ukraine Drone Attack
item.description: Russia said a fire broke out in the Port of Vysotsk area on the Baltic Sea after overnight Ukrainian drone attacks, while a strike on Ukraine’s northern region left 380,000 users without power.

item.title: UK Bears Scars to Economy, Trump Ties Even as War Risks Subside
item.description: The UK economy and London’s relationship with Washington have taken significant blows that are likely to reverberate for months.

item.title: China Central Bank’s Pan Flags AI Risks, Opportunities at IMF
item.description: China’s central bank governor Pan Gongsheng said artificial intelligence is driving a new wave of technological and industrial transformation that brings both opportunities and risks to the global economy.

item.title: Australian Minister Says Fuel Reserves Up, More Shipments on Way
item.description: Australia’s fuel reserves rose in the past week and more is on the way, Energy Minister Chris Bowen said, easing concern of shortages in the import-reliant country.

item.title: Fermi Shares Plunge After Announcing Departure of CEO
item.description: Fermi shares fell as much as 31% in post-market trading after the developer behind a massive planned AI campus in Texas announced the immediate departure of co-founder and Chief Executive Officer Toby Neugebauer.

item.title: Soaring Tungsten Prices Add Impetus to Vietnam Mine Sale Effort
item.description: Some 80 kilometers (50 miles) north of Hanoi, in Thai Nguyen province, a massive open-cut mine tears into the landscape. Ringed by dense, green hills, the vast, stepped crater is raw gray and brown. Along its sides, huge trucks creep along, while a murky pool lies stagnant at the bottom.

item.title: Blue Owl Co-CEOs’ Personal Loans No Longer Backed by Firm Shares
item.description: Blue Owl Capital Inc. Co-Chief Executive Officers Doug Ostrover and Marc Lipschultz have revised the terms of personal loans to remove the company’s shares as collateral, after turmoil in the private credit market hammered the stock’s value in recent months.

item.title: Geothermal Power Firm Fervo Energy Files for IPO
item.description: Fervo Energy Co. filed for an initial public offering, disclosing wider losses as it plans to start generating power from its first geothermal project in Utah later this year.

item.title: KKR-Backed Emergency Services Firm GMR Files for US IPO
item.description: GMR Solutions Inc., the KKR & Co.-backed air and ground emergency medical services company, filed for a US initial public offering as it joins a growing list of firms seeking to tap investors.

item.title: Live Nation Inks $742 Million of Private Debt Tied to Venues
item.description: Live Nation Entertainment Inc. has borrowed about €630 million ($742 million) in the private debt market to help finance its global investment in concert venues, people with knowledge of the matter said.

item.title: Blackstone-Backed Ad Tech Firm Liftoff Mobile Refiles for US IPO
item.description: Liftoff Mobile Inc. filed again for an initial public offering just two months after withdrawing a previous registration.

item.title: Biotech Odyssey, Medical Device Firm Mobia File for US IPOs
item.description: Healthcare companies are lining up to go public with a drug developer and medical device company joining a pool of peers seeking to advance public listing plans amid robust investor demand for biotech stocks.

item.title: Belgium Gets Cut by Moody’s in Reproof at Failure to Cut Debt
item.description: Belgium was lowered one step by Moody’s, underscoring how the struggle to cut one of the biggest budget deficits in Europe is besmirching the country’s status as a borrower.

item.title: OpenAI’s Former Product Chief and Sora Head Leave Company
item.description: OpenAI’s head of science initiatives and the leader for its Sora AI video team are both leaving the company, adding to a recent string of departures while the company reorganizes its sprawling product portfolio.

item.title: AI Chipmaker Cerebras Systems Files Publicly for US IPO
item.description: Cerebras Systems Inc., an artificial intelligence chipmaker and data center operator, filed publicly for an initial public offering months after withdrawing a previous attempt to list.

item.title: Wall Street Rushes to Price Out War Damage as Stocks Hit Records
item.description: Traders spent this week betting that the US conflict with Iran is all but over — driving stocks to records, dumping the dollar and pushing oil to around $90 a barrel.

item.title: Uruguay Central Bank Chief Sees Better Growth in First Quarter
item.description: Uruguay’s economy should bounce back from weak growth in the second half of last year to expand by just under 1% in this year’s first quarter, according to the country’s top central banker.

item.title: Tory Burch Sweetens Terms on Loan to Buy Out General Atlantic
item.description: Luxury retailer Tory Burch LLC sweetened terms on a $700 million leveraged loan intended to help it repurchase private equity firm General Atlantic’s stake.

item.title: Fertilizer Prices Drop as Hormuz Opening Brings Farmers Relief
item.description: Fertilizer prices were dropping sharply Friday after Iran said the Strait of Hormuz was open, bringing relief to farmers even as they still face delays in deliveries.

item.title: Allbirds’ AI Pivot Found A Buyer in the Rebirthed MEME Fund
item.description: A rebirthed meme fund was ready to pounce on Allbird’s AI pivot

item.title: Kazaks Warns Against Assuming ECB’s Next Move Will Be a Hike
item.description: It’s not a given that the European Central Bank’s next move on interest rates will be a hike, according to Governing Council member Martins Kazaks.

item.title: IMF Warns Suspending EU Carbon Market Comes With Huge Risks
item.description: The International Monetary Fund urged the European Union not to pause its emissions trading system, warning it would come with “huge” risks that might jeopardize investment.

item.title: ECB’s Kocher Warns Against Preemptive Rate Action on Uncertainty
item.description: The European Central Bank must avoid knee-jerk policy action over the conflict in the Middle East, according to Governing Council member Martin Kocher.

item.title: Codelco Targets Higher 2027 Output to Reclaim Top Copper Spot
item.description: Codelco is targeting a slight increase in copper output in 2027 as Chile’s state-owned miner looks to reclaim the mantle of world’s biggest supplier of the industrial metal.

item.title: Spain Signs Deals With Brazil as Sánchez Builds Anti-Trump Bloc
item.description: Spain and Brazil signed a flurry of agreements at Pedro Sánchez and Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva’s first bilateral summit, as the Spanish leader seeks to build an alliance to counter US President Donald Trump.

item.title: An Unqualified Good Year For Stocks
item.description: Are we headed back to US underperformance?

item.title: Macklem Warns Against Hiking Too Early or Too Late on Oil Shock
item.description: Bank of Canada Governor Tiff Macklem said central banks around the world are positioned differently when it comes to economic slack and inflation, and their responses to the oil price shock are likely to vary.
